findings.title: Synchronous collector reporting can stall every status command
findings.impact: Every `dscli status` invocation waits for `urlopen` to complete after local discovery, including the empty-worker case. If the collector is slow or unreachable, a command whose primary job is local process inventory can block for up to the 5 second timeout per host, which becomes painful for operator scripts that fan out across many hosts.
findings.fix: Keep local status output on the fast path and move collector publishing behind a much shorter timeout, an opt-in flag, or an asynchronous/background best-effort path that cannot delay the command's local result.
